[Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") is divided into 212 **municipalities** ([Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): ***občine***, singular – *[občina](/wiki/Ob%C4%8Dina "Občina")*), of which 12 have urban (metropolitan) status. Municipalities are further divided into local communities and districts. Slovenia has the largest number of first-level administrative divisions of any country.

The municipalities vary considerably in size and population, from the capital [Ljubljana](/wiki/Ljubljana "Ljubljana") with more than 280,000 inhabitants to [Hodoš](/wiki/Municipality_of_Hodo%C5%A1 "Municipality of Hodoš") with fewer than 400. Urban status is not granted strictly on the basis of population; the smallest urban municipality, [Slovenj Gradec](/wiki/Urban_Municipality_of_Slovenj_Gradec "Urban Municipality of Slovenj Gradec"), has less than half as many inhabitants as the most populous non-urban municipality, [Domžale](/wiki/Municipality_of_Dom%C5%BEale "Municipality of Domžale").

[Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language") is the [official language](/wiki/Official_language "Official language") in all municipalities. [Hungarian](/wiki/Hungarian_language "Hungarian language") is the second official language of three municipalities in [Prekmurje](/wiki/Prekmurje "Prekmurje"): [Dobrovnik](/wiki/Dobrovnik "Dobrovnik")/Dobronak, [Hodoš](/wiki/Hodo%C5%A1 "Hodoš")/Hodos, and [Lendava](/wiki/Lendava "Lendava")/Lendva. [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language") is the second official language of four municipalities (of which one has urban status) in the [Slovene Littoral](/wiki/Slovene_Littoral "Slovene Littoral"): [Ankaran](/wiki/Ankaran "Ankaran")/Ancarano, [Izola](/wiki/Izola "Izola")/Isola, [Koper](/wiki/Koper "Koper")/Capodistria, and [Piran](/wiki/Piran "Piran")/Pirano.

For EU statistical purposes, Slovenian municipalities are classified as "local administrative unit 2" (LAU 2), below the country's 58 administrative units (**upravne enote**),[[1]](#cite_note-1) which are classified LAU 1.

## Names

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipalities_of_Slovenia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Names")]

The Slovene names of the municipalities as territorial/administrative units have the word *občina* 'municipality', followed by a [nominative](/wiki/Nominative_case "Nominative case") form, usually the seat of the municipality; for example, *Občina Ajdovščina* 'Municipality of Ajdovščina'. The word *občina* should be capitalised as *Občina* when it refers to the official designation of the community or the [legal person](/wiki/Legal_person "Legal person") representing the community, whereas it should be uncapitalised if it is meant as a generic term.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) For the urban municipalities, the term used is *mestna občina* ([generic name](/wiki/Generic_term "Generic term")) or *Mestna občina* ([proper name](/wiki/Proper_noun "Proper noun")).[[4]](#cite_note-4)
